entries.back_en: Oh, alas! Oh, by all the gods, m-my stomach. How it
entries.note: 'every god' rendered as 'all the gods'; acceptable

entries.back_en: yearns to break its f-chains... What misfortune! Alas!
entries.note: 'fetters' as 'correntes'; consonant of stutter shifted f->c but fine

entries.back_en: Wh-What inconsideration, to release me so brusquely!
Maroro is a man besieged, thus I proclaim--heURGH?
entries.note: 'so sayeth--he' is third person; target 'proclamo' is first person, clashing with retained 'he'

entries.back_en: O gods, b-but how these backwood trails wound
the spirit and vex the bowels! Have pity...
entries.note: 'this poor soul' omitted but sense preserved

entries.back_en: Perhaps, friend Ukon, perhaps--but to lower myself
so ignobly? U-Unthinkable 'tis...
Exactly! No man of high birth should lower himself
to walk on the dirt like a vagrant commoner.
I-It matters--hURGgh--m-matters not the cost! Even
having fallen from it; grace, we mustn't for--HURG?
To s-sully my own name with such v-vulgar behavior,
don't even think of iAURGH--HRF--!!
I thank thee, dear freEURGH--
Oh, I don't want the flask-- I mean, um...
You can keep it.
Urk... M-Master... Haku, was it? Truly, thou art
a--HEURGH--man of g-great kindness, and
entries.note: word split preserved across control code

entries.back_en: untold HURK--
I pray you dispense--HURP--with thy humility, O master
H-Haku! Never deep enough my gra--AGHtitude--
Without even a thought unto thyself, thou stoopest to
aid poor Maroro! Truly, thou art a bosom frieNGH--!
